nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: Striped honeyeater
nodes.summary: Striped honeyeater（Striped honeyeater，学名 Plectorhyncha lanceolata），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联7个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括沼泽、灌丛。

nodes.name: 昆士蘭州東南部
nodes.summary: 昆士蘭州東南部是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括艾氏琴鸟、Striped honeyeater，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: 阿德萊德平原
nodes.summary: 阿德萊德平原是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Striped honeyeater，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Mallee Woodlands and Shrublands
nodes.summary: Mallee Woodlands and Shrublands是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联10种鸟类，包括Australian owlet-nightjar、Red-backed kingfisher、黄尾黑凤头鹦鹉等10种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 中海岸 (新南威爾斯州)
nodes.summary: 中海岸 (新南威爾斯州)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Striped honeyeater，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 约翰·古尔德
nodes.summary: 约翰·古尔德是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联71种鸟类，包括绿棉凫、凍原豆雁、黄腹角雉等71种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Tuggerah Lake
nodes.summary: Tuggerah Lake是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Striped honeyeater，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 格蘭坪國家公園
nodes.summary: 格蘭坪國家公園是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括Striped honeyeater、蓝脸吸蜜鸟，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The striped honeyeater (Plectorhyncha lanceolata) is a passerine bird of the honeyeater family, Meliphagidae, found in Australia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: While it is found mainly in inland eastern Australia where it inhabits the drier open forest, it is also found in coastal swamp forest from southeast Queensland to the central coast of New South Wales

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: While it is found mainly in inland eastern Australia where it inhabits the drier open forest, it is also found in coastal swamp forest from southeast Queensland to the central coast of New South Wales

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | title = The Coastal Habitat of the Striped Honey-eater (Plectorhyncha lanceolata)| journal = Emu | volume = 23 | pages = 32–33 | publisher = CSIRO | location = Melbourne, Victoria | year = 1923 | issue = 1 | issn = 0158-4197 | doi = 10.1071/MU923032 | url = http://www.publish.csiro.au/mu/pdf/mu923032 | doi-access = free | bibcode = 1923EmuAO..23...32G }} However, the range has expanded, and contemporary accounts give its distribution as eastern Australia from central and southeast Queensland extending inland to southeast South Australia, and along the coast south to the Tuggerah Lakes, New South Wales. Small irruptions associated with weather patterns and habitat changes have been recorded in areas outside its usual range, including the Grampians and the Adelaide Plains. It occurs in a wide variety of habitats, including riparian woodlands such as river red gum, bimble box and black box with an understory of lignum or saltbush; mallee woodland, especially where mixed with thickets of broombush or emu bush; woodlands of native pine growing on sand ridges; and semi-arid scrubland dominated by Acacia including lancewood. On the coast, the striped honeyeater is found in swamp forests of paperbark and Casuarina,{{Cite journal| last = Morris | first = A
